HomePage.title: Agent Workflow Hub
HomePage.hero_title: Build alongside AI, without the chaos.
HomePage.hero_subtitle: A strict, memory-persistent workflow framework for cursor, windsurf, and cline. Eliminate context bloat, hallucination, and UI inconsistency forever.
HomePage.feature_1_desc: Instead of feeding the AI your entire codebase, the protocol uses STATE.md to maintain a lightweight, always-accurate RAM of the current task.
HomePage.feature_1_prob: Chats get too long, slow down, and cost too many tokens.
HomePage.feature_1_sol: A 20-line STATE.md file keeps the AI focused and lightning fast.
HomePage.feature_2_desc: When a chat breaks or gets too long, our One-Click Handoff script packs up the context and sends it to a new session perfectly.
HomePage.feature_2_prob: Starting a new chat means the AI forgets your rules and past bugs.
HomePage.feature_2_sol: Seamless handoffs preserve architectural decisions and lessons learned.
HomePage.feature_3_desc: Enforce a Single Source of Truth for your design system. The AI reads your UI guidelines before writing any frontend code.
HomePage.feature_3_prob: The AI invents new colors and layouts that break your design.
HomePage.feature_3_sol: Strict design tokens ensure pixel-perfect consistency.
HomePage.feature_4_desc: Built-in Git Hooks recursively scan for .env files to prevent API key leaks. Enterprise-grade file writing locks prevent symlink corruption.
HomePage.feature_4_prob: Automated agents accidentally expose secrets or overwrite critical files.
HomePage.feature_4_sol: Multiple safety layers guard your repository during AI operation.
HomePage.portfolio_subtitle: My open-source ecosystem of tools, workflows, and frameworks.
HomePage.project_1_desc: A framework to eliminate context amnesia for AI coding agents.
HomePage.project_2_desc: A modern content management system built with Next.js and Tailwind.
HomePage.project_3_desc: A centralized hub to discover and install Model Context Protocol tools.

ManualPage.title: Documentation
ManualPage.subtitle: Comprehensive guide to setting up and using the Agent Workflow Framework.
ManualPage.problems_p1: When working on longer projects with AI assistants in IDEs, common issues arise:
ManualPage.problems_li1: Context Size: Chats become too long, slowing down responses and increasing token usage.
ManualPage.problems_li2: Context Loss: Starting a new chat causes the AI to forget project rules, structure, and past bug fixes.
ManualPage.problems_li3: Inconsistency: The AI may drift from the project's design system or architectural guidelines.
ManualPage.problems_p2: This protocol provides a structure to manage these issues:
ManualPage.problems_sol1: State Tracking (STATE.md): Keeps a summary of the current task and past learnings.
ManualPage.problems_sol2: Session Management: A script to help summarize and hand over context to new chat sessions.
ManualPage.problems_sol3: NotebookLM Integration: Optional tool to offload large documentation to NotebookLM.
ManualPage.notebooklm_p1: You can use NotebookLM to handle large documentation instead of pasting it into the IDE chat. The framework provides a script (./ai-protocol.sh install-mcp) to set up the NotebookLM MCP.
ManualPage.notebooklm_li1: Reduces token usage in the IDE.
ManualPage.notebooklm_li2: Keeps the IDE chat context focused on code.
ManualPage.notebooklm_li3: Uses Google's RAG for querying large documents.
ManualPage.security_p1: The protocol includes basic safeguards for running AI agents locally:
ManualPage.security_li1: File Operations: Uses randomized temporary files and OS-level write locks to prevent accidental overwrites.
ManualPage.security_li2: Secret Scanning: Git pre-commit hooks check for .env files to prevent accidental commits.
ManualPage.structure_code:
/ (Root Project)
├── .ai/
│   ├── STATE.md                    # Current active task
│   ├── REFLECTIONS.md              # Lessons learned & bugs
│   ├── DECISIONS.md                # Architecture Records
│   ├── templates/                  # Blank templates
│   ├── prompts/                    # Example prompts
│   └── docs/                       # Project documentation
├── ai-protocol.sh                   # Helper script
└── SKILL.md                         # Core instructions
ManualPage.guidelines_li1: API Contract-First: Verify API schemas before writing calls.
ManualPage.guidelines_li2: Test-Driven: Write tests for complex logic first.
ManualPage.guidelines_li3: Security: Do not hardcode secrets. Use .env.
ManualPage.themes_p1: The .ai/templates/ui/ folder contains standard UI guidelines:
ManualPage.themes_li2: Data Heavy: Tremor style, table-focused, clean data visualization
ManualPage.themes_li3: Minimal: Vercel style, black & white, high contrast, simple
ManualPage.themes_li4: Vibrant: HeroUI style, colorful, rounded corners
ManualPage.script_p1: A CLI tool is included to help manage the workspace:
ManualPage.script_code:
# Check safety rules and file sizes
./ai-protocol.sh check

# Backup current STATE.md and create a fresh one
./ai-protocol.sh clean

# Generate a prompt for handing off to a new session
./ai-protocol.sh handoff
ManualPage.workflow_li2: Copy the text and paste it into your AI chat as the first message.
ManualPage.workflow_li3: The AI will read .ai/STATE.md and know exactly what to do next.
ManualPage.workflow_li5: Clear your IDE chat (start a new session).
ManualPage.workflow_li6: Paste the generated prompt into the new chat to continue seamlessly.
